reason: Current PRD: self-built Midway.js main backend, PostgreSQL, tenant_id isolation, admin stack open for PR1.
Current MVP PR roadmap aligned to self-built Midway.js backend and PR0/PR1 sequencing.
Backend guideline entry point for the self-built Midway.js/PostgreSQL implementation.
Backend module layout for core tenant/auth/database primitives plus platform, merchant, consumer, order, rental, payment, and funds modules.
Database and tenant isolation contracts: tenant_id, tenant context, PR0 query guard, raw SQL limits, production sync guard, and RLS path.
Backend error handling expectations for tenant, auth, payment, fulfillment, and platform operations.
Logging and audit guidance for tenant-aware operations and sensitive-data handling.
Backend quality gates and forbidden patterns for the self-built Midway backend.
Frontend guideline entry point for uni-app C-side and future admin implementation.
Frontend layout guidance for C-side uni-app and future admin surfaces.
Component design guidance for reusable product, cart, order, rental, and admin UI components.
Composable/hook guidance for API access, tenant context, and cross-page state.
State guidance for tenant-scoped cart, user session, order flow, and UI state.
Type safety guidance for API contracts, order models, and rental/retail product types.
Frontend quality gates and forbidden patterns for implementation.
Cross-layer data flow guidance for product, order, tenant, payment, and extension flows.
Reuse guidance before creating shared tenant/order/product/payment helpers.
Framework comparison history; Midway.js remains relevant as the chosen main backend family.
ORM/database comparison history and PostgreSQL/RLS safety conclusions.
Historical reference only: explains why cool-admin v8 was considered, but it is no longer the target architecture.
Historical reference for extension ideas; current plan uses project-owned modules and Strategy extension points, not cool-admin runtime plugins.
C-side frontend stack decision: uni-app Vue3, wot-design-uni, Pinia, and tenant headers.
Historical admin/C-side research; admin implementation remains open for PR1 and should not assume cool-admin-vue as current target.
Order model and dual-layer state machine research for PR3/PR5/PR7.
Payment/funds research for PR6; adapt implementation to project-owned payment Strategy modules.
PR0 self-built Midway.js application bootstrap.
PR0 tenant context helper using AsyncLocalStorage.
PR0 project-owned tenant query guard skeleton.
Architecture guard for self-built Midway backend markers and forbidden cool-admin runtime dependencies.
Real PostgreSQL tenant guard tests using rent_test with clear skip behavior when PostgreSQL is unavailable.
Staged three-surface delivery model for future implementation tasks
